name: San Vicente de la Barquera – circular from Playa La Espina
name_original: San Vicente de la Barquera – circular desde Playa La Espina
region: Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre
region_intro: Hiking in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre offers diverse coastal and inland landscapes within Cantabria, Spain. The region features a dramatic coastline with cliffs ranging from 10 to 50 meters, extensive dune systems, and beaches like Oyambre and Merón. Inland, the terrain transitions to ridges, meadows, and native forests, including the ecological enclave of Monte Corona. Significant estuaries and marshes, such as those of Río de La Rabia and San Vicente de la Barquera, punctuate the landscape, providing rich biodiversity. Best hiking trails in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre The most popular hiking route is San Vicente de la Barquera – circular from Playa La Espina, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 32 minutes to complete. This easy route offers coastal views and is suitable for beginners. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Playa de Fuentes – View of the Cantabrian Coast loop from Parque Natural de Oyambre, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.4 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the coast and diverse scenery. Local hikers also love the Playa de Merón loop from San Vicente de la Barquera, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through coastal dunes and beaches, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre is defined by coastal paths, extensive dune systems, and marshy estuaries. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Lic-Rias Occidentales Y Duna De Oyambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre, offering a diverse range of experiences from coastal walks to inland explorations. The komoot community has explored more than 1300 routes in the area.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in this region?
A: Hikers in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre will experience a dramatic interplay of coastal and inland features. The region boasts spectacular cliffs, extensive dune systems and beaches like Oyambre and Merón, biodiverse estuaries and marshes such as Río de La Rabia and San Vicente de la Barquera, and inland ridges, meadows, and native forests, including the important ecological enclave of Monte Corona.

Q: Are there easy, family-friendly hiking options in the Oyambre Natural Park?
A: Yes, the region offers numerous easy trails suitable for families and beginners. Approximately half of the routes are classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e998460517/san-vicente-de-la-barquera-circular-from-playa-la-espina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera – circular from Playa La Espina&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.7 km long and offers pleasant coastal views.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see near the hiking trails?
A: Along the trails, you can discover several interesting landmarks. In San Vicente de la Barquera, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint and Church of Santa María de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2287761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Maza&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features beautiful spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes Beach&lt;/a&gt; and scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Zapedo marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For a moderate experience with panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6973095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Playa de Fuentes – View of the Cantabrian Coast loop from Parque Natural de Oyambre&lt;/a&gt;. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40379942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Merón loop from San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through coastal dunes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: The Oyambre Natural Park is enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, ideal for hiking. Summer is also popular, especially for combining hikes with beach activities, though it can be busier. Winters are generally mild, but some coastal paths might be exposed to stronger winds.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Lic-Rías Occidentales y Duna de Oyambre?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning coastal views, the diversity of landscapes from beaches to forests, and the well-preserved natural environment of the Oyambre Natural Park.

Q: Are there opportunities for water sports in the area?
A: Beyond hiking, the extensive beaches like Oyambre and Merón are renowned for water sports such as surfing. The biodiverse estuaries and marshy areas, including those of Río de La Rabia and San Vicente de la Barquera, also offer opportunities for activities like canoeing and rafting.

Q: Can I find trails that offer significant elevation changes for a more challenging hike?
A: While many routes are easy to moderate, there are options for more seasoned hikers seeking elevation. For example, the region offers routes that feature significant ascents and varied terrain, such as the longer circular expeditions that explore the interior ridges and valleys, providing a more challenging experience.

Q: Is the Camino de Santiago accessible through this region?
A: Yes, a section of the historic Way of St. James (Camino de Santiago) passes directly along the coast through Oyambre Natural Park, offering pilgrims and hikers a unique experience that combines spiritual journey with the natural beauty of the Cantabrian coastline.

Q: Are there any unique geological features to observe while hiking?
A: The region is recognized for its geological significance. The estuaries of San Vicente de la Barquera and La Rabia are particularly notable, with the Ría de San Vicente being one of Cantabria's geological points of interest due to its complex fault system. Hikers can observe dramatic cliffs and extensive dune formations as well.
tips: San Vicente de la Barquera is one of the last Cantabrian towns before Asturias. Its old town is charming, with its houses and monuments. In fact, the center of this city has been declared a Site of Cultural Interest in Cantabria since 1987. Do not hesitate to visit the church of Santa María de los Ángeles, the castle and the remains of the walls. The Maza bridge, 600 meters long and with 28 arches, dates from the 16th century and was built by order of the Catholic Monarchs. The Parral bridge dates back to the 18th century. Its beautiful beaches attract many surfers. Tidal changes produce some impressive waves!

It was built in 1210, after the granting of jurisdiction to the town of San Vicente de la Barquera (Cantabria) by Alfonso VIII, although a more legendary origin is supposed to be in the middle of the 8th century, when the Asturian king Alfonso I carried out the first repopulation of the town.

The old town of San Vicente de la Barquera is ideal for a trip back in time. Sometimes you can hear the bagpipes playing in the small streets. The old buildings are majestic and impressive. Next to the church, there is a viewpoint with a panoramic view of the sea and the mountains.

Beautiful hike on the Camino del Norte. The paths are each marked with yellow arrows or on the ground with the typical Camino shell. There are various hostels for pilgrims or possibly hotels. I chose Hotel. Overnight in Colombres at Hotel Valbanera. Hotel recommended, right on the Way of St. James. I recommend staying in San Vicente or to Unquera. Follow the coastal path, not the forest path. The coastal path has a lot more to offer.
